title: Iran Formally Rejects Ceasefire Negotiations
detail: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi formally stated Iran rejects all ceasefire talks, declaring 'We never asked for a ceasefire' and 'The United States is responsible for all the consequences of this war.' Iran's position is that the war must end in a way that prevents future attacks, but Tehran refuses to negotiate with Washington.

title: Strait of Hormuz Crisis: 20M bbl/day Backed Up
detail: The IRGC warned no oil will pass through the Strait of Hormuz and threatened $200/barrel oil. 20 million barrels per day are backed up at the strait, with only 5 million finding alternative pipeline routes. This represents the largest energy supply disruption since the 1970s oil crisis. The US Navy announced tanker escort operations when militarily possible.

title: Oman, Qatar, China Positioned as Mediators
detail: International Crisis Group reported that Oman, Qatar, and China have positioned themselves as potential mediators for back-channel dialogue to find an off-ramp in the Middle East war. Pre-war negotiations in Oman and Geneva where Iran agreed to IAEA verification of uranium stockpiles have been derailed by the military conflict.
